title: parahebe brassii
intro: Parahebe brassii, also known as the New Zealand Parahebe, is a small, flowering plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The Parahebe brassii plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues. The health benefits of Parahebe brassii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
